hae5d36e3gca4fg41c2g9f43gd303376a32eb: I have no more to say. Even old hay tastes better than your idle chatter.
h322eee9eg6cb4g4e64g87bfg832a7b8ba4c3: Another one. What are you under there, then? Ant? Aurochs?
h16dd4b04g9fb3g44a3gb597g922372e0911f: The keepers of this place. They change their form to fit their need.
h16d17fe7gca72g4250gb466g2b11396ea808: They become birds, to hunt on the wing. Bears, to tear flesh with teeth. But for me - only oxen. Only same old hay.
h39215f3dg1957g4d85gbb26g4c2e57d7146d: And I cannot even eat it, because the horned ones hide their shiny bits inside.
h561ab144ge7deg44aeg9c89gb7af34583518: 'Shiny bits'? The tieflings put something in your hay?
h4a97be65g3e22g44d1gbb71g1b32d8fedb70: Hiding things, thinking they are clever. Not clever.
h06a102f1g2fd6g4156gbf8ag0fcf01f11aab: Sometimes I eat the shiny bits, and they have to go looking for them on the other end.
hfb721a72g8603g465cg87e2gfbdaba01b404: Cheer up - you do a noble thing, carrying burdens for those who can't.
h200110acg7fefg4b00g82b6g257a48a73107: Hmph. When the horned ones leave this place, I will lie in the back, and you will pull. Noble one.
h801a68bage5c1g42acgbb4cg43fe10d82d80: Your kind give us your burdens. But who will carry ours?
h4ed3137bg9bd2g43e9g9634g55600a0160e6: Maybe you could learn to change as we druids do.
h3fc18ad0gf3f5g4460g9f83g4352c87a5673: Yes. Yes. I would change into a two-legs like you. But how?
hd8b99ea4g82cdg4691g943bgbdc75a403cc5: You mean when the keepers stand and sleep, all at once? My kind learned to do this long ago.
h5a8f8243gb8deg4bf8gbe4dg3fa4c6f1bfe3: Perhaps we are the true keepers. I will learn to walk on two legs, friend. Then perhaps I will teach them.
h3e8982f2gd930g48d2g939cgcc827e209c33: Surely you could pick a better form than mine.
h1ded206ege5dfg436fg8a5cgf83434ddaf84: It is not the form - it is the freedom. Your kind walk free, and other beasts carry your burdens.
h26d26f61gc0e9g429fg9598g4ebff6051cc6: To live like you, I could learn to be ugly.
h2ccd29a4g55aag4dc0gb8d2g09a949d16eec: No - I will be better. I have studied the keepers here.
hc2294ca4g312bg4b53g8c4cg9d4b9dc8b485: One day, I will stride as you do. And perhaps you will carry my burdens.
ha8683a7fg88d9g4a50g8aa8g7c998dc27198: Big, and slow? The keepers of this place choose different forms. Better ones.
hf7dcdba6g5506g46c8gb86agcfe89389848f: Ant? Are you calling me small?
h38a384abg4826g4a3ag827eg9a1f16ed6c2f: If you do not like your form, then choose another. That is what the keepers of this place do.
h62751371g77f0g483eg97ffga0e937884d25: You have the smell - but I do not think so. You are not like the keepers here.
